creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
videos.summary: Double iframe sandboxing nests one iframe within another to enforce security boundaries without sacrificing functionality. The outer iframe loads a lightweight script from a controlled subdomain, which then loads the actual app via srcdoc into an inner frame, preventing CSP bypasses, storage collisions, and sandbox escapes while maintaining origin-specific storage isolation.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: WorkOS built Studio, an internal workspace where non-technical users ask business questions in natural language against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion. The LLM generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, making results deterministic and cheap on subsequent runs.

videos.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
videos.summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard introduced by Google that enables AI agents to interact with web services through structured, typed tools instead of visual scraping. It provides both declarative HTML-based and imperative JavaScript APIs for registering actions, currently in early preview on Chrome 146.

videos.title: Your Attention Is the Bottleneck, Not Your Agents — Zack Proser, WorkOS
videos.summary: Zack Proser argues that human attention, not agent speed, is the limiting factor in AI-assisted development. He proposes a sustainable multi-layer architecture combining signal agents for notification filtering, verification gates for safety, self-improvement via conversation history analysis, and biometric feedback integration to prevent developer burnout.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is a meta-harness that orchestrates multiple coding models and agents such as Claude Code, Codex, and Pi as interchangeable workers. It enables workflow routing where one agent can plan a task, hand it to Claude Code for implementation, then route the output to Codex for review, with governance and cross-device session support.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: A livestream analysis of Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 model, which was pulled from availability three days after launch due to a US government export-control directive. The creator benchmarks the model's performance and compares its capabilities and pricing against Claude Opus 4.8.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source command-line tool that enables rapid development and production deployment of AI agents. It provides sandboxed code execution environments, agent identity management, and audit trails for production-grade AI agent systems.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin announces a live Q&A stream focused on AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The session will be held on Saturday mornings at 9 AM CT with primary YouTube video content dropping Wednesday evenings at 7 PM CT.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explains how to build proactive AI agents and self-improving company systems. Covers proactive AI loops, autonomous advertising optimization and SEO use cases, and references tools like Loopany for memory and tooling that enable these autonomous loops.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature and autonomous coding capabilities, comparing them to Ralph-loop 2.0 and exploring practical setup and prompting techniques. The video covers goal-buddy functionality and mission exploration for long-horizon coding tasks.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: OpenAI Symphony is a new AI coding framework or tool demonstrated by AI Jason. The video covers Symphony's functionality, implementation via a harness setup with auto-testing, and workflow configuration using WORKFLOW.md.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason reviews agent frameworks and techniques including autoresearch, AutoAgent, Claude's 3-layer memory system, and self-evolving mechanisms in Hermes and Openclaw. The video demonstrates practical approaches to building autonomous AI agents.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series comprising Nano, Super, and Ultra models. The series combines hybrid Mamba transformer, Latent Mixture of Experts, and MTP architectures.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a language model that shifts from full attention to sparse attention (MSA, MiniMax Sparse Attention). The model introduces tiled KV cache optimizations and I/O improvements for efficient memory access during prefill and decode stages of inference.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Pi is an agent framework with a distinctive harness layer architecture that enables multiple use cases. The video compares Pi's design against competitor agent products like Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Antigravity.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Huawei announced the Tau Scaling Law as a competitive response in the hardware race between China and the USA. The strategy leverages EUV and DUV lithography alongside packaging and logic folding techniques to advance semiconductor capabilities.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discuss how to navigate the job market in 2026 as AI systems screen resumes and hiring processes evolve. The video argues that personal networking, building a personal brand through blogging and open source work, and hands-on project development remain essential differentiators for junior developers despite AI disruption.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents five best practices for deploying TypeScript AI agents to production: streaming tool call events for UI responsiveness, layered authentication with API Gateway and Cognito, type-safe database access with tenant isolation, guardrails with cost budgets and model routing, and observability via metrics and OpenTelemetry tracing. The patterns are demonstrated using Strands Agents SDK on AWS infrastructure.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: Tutorial on Infrastructure as Code using AWS CDK with Python to build a serverless REST API. Covers building with DynamoDB, Lambda, and API Gateway, comparing CDK to CloudFormation and Terraform, and deploying infrastructure with a single command.

videos.title: Stop AI Hallucinations With These 5 Techniques
videos.summary: The video demonstrates five techniques to reduce AI hallucinations in agent systems: semantic tool selection to filter context, graph RAG for precise queries, multi-agent validation to catch failures, symbolic guardrails implemented in code rather than prompts, and runtime steering for self-correction. Each technique is shown with before-and-after demos using a travel agent built with Strands Agents framework.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech examines whether Google engineers adopt "vibe coding" practices and compares code generation vibes across OpenAI Codex and Claude. The video explores code generation adoption at Google across 2024, 2025, and 2026 timelines.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses whether Malus, a company or product, is ending open source development practices. The video examines how Malus works and its impact on open source maintainers, framing the debate around whether the company is "evil" or simply changing the open source landscape.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how her AI performance management platform competes against legacy enterprise software giants by building a comprehensive platform from day one rather than a single feature. She shares insights on enterprise sales strategy, the importance of customer trust and brand validation, and hiring philosophy as a competitive advantage.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that rabid fans and single customer conversations are unreliable signals of real product-market fit. He advocates for embedded customer relationships over formal interviews and emphasizes betting on the trajectory of AI models rather than their current state.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird, founder of Koah Labs, argues that ad monetization is the superior model for AI tools over subscriptions, and identifies human trust rather than technology as the primary bottleneck for AI agent adoption. He discusses decision frameworks learned from competitive sailing for making high-stakes choices with incomplete information.

videos.title: I Hit $1M ARR in 117 Days. Bootstrapped to $10M. Here's My Playbook | Chatbase, Yasser Elsaid
videos.summary: Yasser Elsaid, founder of Chatbase, discusses how he bootstrapped to $10M ARR without funding. The interview covers his playbook for reaching $1M ARR in 117 days, including strategies on product-led growth, SEO, pricing experimentation, churn reduction, and founder decisions around raising capital and team structure.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
